relevance_explanation: FSI supports space research, development, and education activities at UCF and across Florida, managing several key labs and projects.
Headquartered at UCF, FSGC is a NASA-funded organization that coordinates space-related research and education programs across Florida universities.
A multidisciplinary group of faculty and students researching spacecraft, telescopes, meteorites, and laboratories to explore the solar system.

official_name: The Stephen W. Hawking Center for Microgravity Research and Education
relevance_explanation: A joint venture between UCF and Space Florida that conducts and facilitates research in microgravity sciences, including ground-based and flight experiments.

official_name: Center for Lunar & Asteroid Surface Science (CLASS)
relevance_explanation: CLASS focus on the intersection of surface science and surface exploration of the Moon and asteroids as part of the NASA SSERVI network.
Managed by FSI, this facility develops high-fidelity regolith simulants for testing space hardware and conducting planetary research.
A collaborative space at FSI where students conduct experiments and analyze data related to asteroids and other small solar system bodies.

official_name: Center for Science, Technology and Advanced Research in Space (C-STARS)
relevance_explanation: A multidisciplinary research hub focused on advancing space manufacturing and biotechnology in microgravity.
Based at the College of Medicine, this program pioneers research and clinical services related to human health in space and extreme environments.

official_name: Human Research Program for Civilian Spaceflight (HRP-C)
relevance_explanation: An initiative at UCF addressing the health, safety, and performance of civilian participants in commercial spaceflight missions.
Focuses on orbital mechanics, astrodynamics, and the control of robotic systems for space exploration applications.
Conducts high-pressure reacting flow research with applications in advanced aerospace propulsion systems, including rockets and hypersonics.

official_name: Center for Advanced Turbomachinery and Energy Research (CATER)
relevance_explanation: Focuses on turbomachinery and advanced technologies for aviation and space propulsion, including hypersonic research platforms.
An RSO based out of the Department of Physics that engages students in observational astronomy and space-related outreach.

official_name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS UCF)
relevance_explanation: Promotes space exploration through student projects, leadership development, and education about the benefits of space.

official_name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA UCF)
relevance_explanation: A project-based aerospace engineering student organization and student chapter of the national AIAA, focusing on professional development and engineering projects.
UCF's official competitive rocketry team that designs, builds, and launches experimental rockets for international competitions.
A student center for satellite and space systems development, providing opportunities for students to design and build CubeSats and other payloads.
Participates in the NASA Robotic Mining Competition (Lunabotics), designing and building robotic rovers for lunar mining simulations.
A seminar series and group where students and faculty discuss the latest research in planetary sciences.
The university's astronomical observatory, serving as a hub for both educational observation and community outreach in astronomy.

official_name: Society of Women in Space Exploration (SWISE) UCF Chapter
relevance_explanation: An RSO that supports and promotes women and gender minorities in space exploration fields through networking and professional development.
A sub-team within AIAA UCF that competes in the international AIAA Design/Build/Fly aircraft competition.
A project within SEDS UCF focused on developing custom solid rocket motor propellants for high-power rocketry.

official_name: Student Spaceflight Experiments Program (SSEP) at UCF
relevance_explanation: A collaborative student group that designs and flies experiments to the International Space Station as part of a national program.
Participates in the development of advanced microwave remote sensing instruments for NASA and NOAA, focusing on Earth observation.
While broad, the club frequently participates in space-related competitions such as the NASA Robotic Mining Competition.
An earlier chapter of the international Mars Society that advocated for Mars exploration; currently inactive on campus.
The original research group founded in 1992 that eventually evolved into the Stephen W. Hawking Center for Microgravity Research.
A competitive VexU robotics team at UCF; strongly adjacent to aerospace engineering students and planetary robotics projects.
